free_text: Measurements of neutron induced fission cross section
Bombarding a tritium-titanium target by 750 keV
The target was deposited on a copper plate of 0.25 mm
The neutrons of 14.9, 14.5, 14.0 and 13.5 MeV were
obtained at the emission angles of 0, 65, 100 and
150 deg with respect to the deuteron beam.
The counter gas was 90% Ar and 10% C-H4 mixture.
The samples were prepared by the electrodeposition on
the platinum plates, which were 36 mm in diam. and
0.3 or 0.4 mm in thickness, and sintered into the
oxide to fix them on the plates.
The diameter of the deposits was 25 mm.
The isotopic compositions of the samples were
determined by means of the alpha spectroscopy with a
high resolution surface barrier detector (except for
The 235U sample and another one were mounted back to
back on the midplane of the fission chamber.
- extrapolation from the tail of the fission spectrum
- self-absorption of fission fragments in samples,
- attenuation of neutron flux by backing plates,
- difference in solid angle subtended by samples to
target between the 235U sample and another one
